name: Alamosa State Bank
description_short: Alamosa State Bank is a community bank chartered in 1907, offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and credit products from locations in Colorado.
description_long:
Alamosa State Bank was founded in 1907 as Alamosa National Bank with $25,000 in capital and has operated continuously for over 115 years. The bank relocated to its current Main Street location in 1920 and positions itself as a community-focused financial institution serving both personal and business customers in the Alamosa, Colorado area. The bank maintains a routing number (102100853) and advertises a commitment to customer relationships with the tagline "A Real Community Bank."

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, personal investments, wire transfers, debit/ATM cards, prepaid gift and travel cards, and safe deposit boxes. For business customers, they provide business checking, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, health savings accounts, wire transfers, and business credit and debit cards. Loan products span both personal lending and business lending categories. Digital banking capabilities include personal and business online banking, bill pay, external funds transfers, e-statements, and mobile banking services.

Alamosa State Bank distinguishes itself through its long operational history and local community presence. The bank emphasizes fraud prevention and account security in its messaging and advertises 24-hour phone access (EZ Access) for customers. They offer customer education resources for both personal and business clients and maintain a physical branch with drive-up and commercial lane services at 2225 Main Street in Alamosa, Colorado.

As a traditional community bank, Alamosa State Bank appears well-suited for customers seeking personalized banking relationships and local presence, though the website provides limited detail on specific rates, fees, loan terms, or competitive advantages versus larger regional or national institutions. The lack of detailed product information online suggests customers may need to contact the bank directly for pricing and terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Long-established community bank with 115+ years of operational history since 1907
Comprehensive product suite including personal/business checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and investments
24-hour phone access (EZ Access) for customer support
Multiple digital banking channels: online banking, bill pay, mobile banking, e-statements
Physical branch with drive-up and commercial lane services in Alamosa
Emphasis on fraud prevention and account security messaging
Health Savings Account (HSA) offerings for business customers
cons: Website lacks specific information on interest rates, APRs, and fee structures
Limited detail on loan terms, minimum deposits, or product eligibility requirements
No transparent comparison of rates versus regional or national competitors
Website content is sparse on product details, requiring customers to contact bank directly
No information on FDIC insurance coverage or other regulatory protections
best_for: Local Alamosa, Colorado residents seeking personalized community banking relationships
Small businesses in the Alamosa area needing business checking and lending services
Customers who value local branch access and personal service over online-only banking
diagnosis: Alamosa State Bank is best for individuals and small businesses in the Alamosa, Colorado area seeking traditional community banking with personal service and local branch access. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on rates, fees, and loan terms, requiring prospective customers to contact the bank directly for pricing information and product comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
